title: Gate LLM synthesis to significant commits; filter machine-generated noise from diffs
context: Engineering memory system was synthesizing every commit, including trivial changes and machine-generated files (lockfiles, build artifacts). This wasted LLM tokens on changes with no design intent and polluted context windows with large diffs.
decision: Added significance gating before synthesis: (1) exclude machine-generated paths (lockfiles, dist/, node_modules/, snapshots, .min.js, .map) at git level before diff assembly, (2) check structural/churn criteria to skip trivial commits, (3) fall back to fast heuristic for non-significant changes, (4) use LLM only for substantive commits, (5) allow --force re-synthesis to override.
consequences: LLM synthesis now used only for commits with real design intent, reducing token spend and latency
Observability: logging shows LLM vs heuristic breakdown per backfill run
Large lockfiles/build artifacts no longer truncate meaningful code diffs
--force flag enables re-synthesis if heuristic decision was wrong
Read-side token caps added (WHY_CAP, QUERY_HITS) to prevent context pollution from large result sets
alternatives_rejected: Synthesize everything unconditionally (cost unsustainable, context pollution)
No re-synthesis capability (inflexible once decision is cached)
Silent truncation of results (user loses signal about withheld data)
